data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN (updated 2023); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; CGMFPFED Portal

Q25.r: The scheme's official notification and Outcome Budget explicitly mandate livelihood and income output indicators. Tracked indicators include: employment generated (210,000 direct + secondary), income per collector household per year (₹42,000), cooperative formation and membership count, processing unit establishment, and NTFP volume collected per species. The scheme's core purpose as stated in Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN is income improvement for tribal NTFP collectors — the economic pathway from forest commons to household income is explicitly articulated throughout the scheme design.
Q25.src: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN (updated 2023); Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

observed.calc: Observed ΔY = Average income under scheme (₹42,000/collector/year) minus pre-scheme baseline (₹18,000/year from informal trade) = ₹24,000/HH/year. Both figures independently verified by CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 through transaction records (₹42,000) and comparative baseline (₹18,000). Economic Survey 2024-25 corroborates both figures.
observed.interp: PASS — Observed ΔY ₹24,000/HH/year ≥ ₹3,000 threshold (8× threshold). Governs.
observed.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 ('income improvement 133%', income ₹42k vs ₹18k baseline verified through transaction records); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

projected.calc: Projected ΔY from DPR not separately available; scheme is operational and observed data governs.
projected.interp: skip — Observed data governs; projected data not required.

Q26.r: Observed ΔY of ₹24,000/HH/year governs per framework rule (observed data takes precedence over projected). Data source is CAG-audited income records (₹42,000 average income) against a verified baseline of ₹18,000 from informal trade. CAG audit verified this through actual transaction records in CGMFPFED collector database.
docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (MFPT section — income analysis)

Q27.r: Three distinct livelihood pathways explicitly articulated in scheme documents: (1) Direct NTFP collection with Minimum Purchase Price (MPP) support — core pathway enabling 185,000 collectors to sell tendu, sal, bamboo, honey, herbs at guaranteed floor prices (tendu ₹4,500/MT, sal ₹8,200/MT, honey ₹250/L) per Official Notification; (2) Cooperative collective marketing — 35 producer cooperatives enable collective bargaining and premium pricing above MPP, explicitly constituted and supported under scheme (₹40 cr cooperative support budget component); (3) Value addition and processing enterprises — 45 processing units (honey extraction, herb drying) convert raw MFP to value-added products, creating secondary employment for 25,000 workers beyond primary collectors, explicitly funded and mandated in scheme design.
Q27.src: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN (updated 2023); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q28.r: Institutional offtake mechanism operational: (1) Minimum Purchase Price (MPP) — state government-mandated floor prices for major NTFP species (tendu ₹4,500/MT, sal ₹8,200/MT, honey ₹250/liter), implemented and verified by CAG. (2) CGMFPFED (Chhattisgarh Minor Forest Produce Federation) — government agency that acts as institutional buyer and market guarantor, operating the buyer platform on its portal. (3) 35 cooperatives with collective bargaining rights further stabilise offtake. This constitutes 'state government procurement scheme for tribal/community produce' and 'government agency offtake', qualifying under both criteria.
Q28.src: Official Notification No. 6-MFPT/2000/CGN (MPP specified); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (MPP implementation verified); CGMFPFED Portal (buyer platform operational)

Q29.r: Scheme MIS and CAG audit track monetary/income metrics only: income per collector (₹42,000), total trade value (₹3,620 cr), employment count, cooperative membership, processing volume. While NTFP includes food items (honey, bamboo shoots) and medicinal herbs that provide non-monetary subsistence value, the scheme does not explicitly track food security months, nutritional diversity (food species count), livestock feeding days, or medicinal plant access frequency as formal MIS indicators. Near-threshold flag NOT triggered: Observed ΔY = ₹24,000/HH/year is 8× the observed threshold (₹3,000), well beyond the ±20% near-threshold range.
Q29.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 (indicator framework); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (audit metrics); CGMFPFED Portal (MIS statistics)

pass_verification.enh_T3.5_flag: ok — ΔY = ₹24,000 is 8× observed threshold; near-threshold flag not applicable
